issues.title: Daily Log page missing loading, error, and empty states
issues.description: The Daily Log page renders content without skeleton loaders during data fetch, shows no error UI on fetch failure, and has no contextual empty state when no logs exist. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires skeleton loaders within 100ms, contextual empty states with CTA, and inline retry on failure. This violates criteria 11, 12, and the Global Loading/Empty/Error standards.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, in the main DailyLog component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component renders data directly without handling isLoading, error, or empty data array states. There are no skeleton placeholders, no error banner with retry, and no contextual empty state illustration. AFTER: 1) When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader matching the page layout: 4 metric card skeletons in a row (height 96px, border-radius 12px, pulsing animation using opacity 0.3 to 0.7 over 1.5s), plus 6 table row skeletons (height 48px each). 2) When error is truthy, render an inline error banner with a red-tinted background, the error message text, and a 'Retry' button that calls the refetch function. Do NOT render a full-page error. 3) When data is loaded but the array is empty, render: a Calendar icon (64px, color #9CA3AF), heading text 'No daily logs yet', description text 'The daily log is your project official record. Start documenting site conditions, crew activity, and weather.', and a primary CTA button 'Start Today Log' (background #F47820, white text, border-radius 8px, height 40px) that opens the create log form. 4) When data is loaded but a filter/search returns no results, show 'No logs match your filters' with a 'Clear filters' button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Field Capture page lacks ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation
issues.description: The Field Capture page has no aria-labels on icon buttons (camera, voice, tag), no aria-live region for upload status changes, no role attributes on the photo grid, and interactive elements are not keyboard focusable. This violates Global Accessibility standards requiring axe-core zero critical violations, keyboard navigability, and screen reader support.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, in the main FieldCapture component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: Icon-only buttons for camera capture, voice notes, and tagging have no aria-label attributes. The photo grid uses div elements without role='list' or role='listitem'. Upload progress and status changes are not announced to screen readers. Tab key does not focus interactive photo cards. AFTER: 1) Add aria-label='Capture photo' to the camera button, aria-label='Record voice note' to the voice button, aria-label='Add tags' to the tag button, and aria-label='Filter captures' to any filter controls. 2) Add role='list' and aria-label='Field captures' to the photo grid container. Add role='listitem' to each photo card div. 3) Add an aria-live='polite' region that announces upload status: 'Uploading X photos', 'Upload complete', or 'Upload failed'. 4) Add tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handler (Enter/Space to open detail) to each photo card. 5) Add aria-label to the main page container: aria-label='Field capture management'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety page not responsive below 768px, metric cards overflow
issues.description: The Safety page renders metric cards in a fixed multi-column grid that overflows horizontally on mobile (375px). Touch targets on incident log action buttons are smaller than 44x44px. The inspection table does not collapse to cards on mobile. This violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Responsive standards and the Safety section criterion 11 requiring mobile-optimized incident reporting.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, in the main Safety component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The metric cards container uses a fixed gridTemplateColumns like 'repeat(4, 1fr)' or 'repeat(5, 1fr)' that causes horizontal overflow on screens under 768px. Action buttons in table rows have small click areas (likely 24px or 32px icons). The incidents table renders as a standard table on all screen sizes. AFTER: 1) Change the metric cards grid to use CSS that responds to viewport: at widths below 768px use gridTemplateColumns 'repeat(2, 1fr)', and below 480px use 'repeat(1, 1fr)'. Use a media query approach via a useMediaQuery hook or a responsive style object with minmax. Since we use inline styles, add a state variable like `const isMobile = window.innerWidth < 768` with a resize listener, then conditionally set gridTemplateColumns. 2) Ensure all action buttons (edit incident, view details, create observation) have minimum dimensions of 44x44px by setting minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44, and padding appropriately. 3) On mobile (below 768px), replace the incidents table with a card-based list where each incident is a card showing severity color bar on left, incident type, date, and status badge. Keep the table view for desktop. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Daily Log missing weather auto-population from weather cache
issues.description: The Daily Log create form requires manual weather entry despite the weather_cache table and useWeather hook existing in the codebase. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA criterion 2 requires weather auto-population from the weather API based on project location, with an 'Auto' badge on auto-populated fields. The 'Same as yesterday' button (criterion 16) is also missing.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, in the daily log creation form section (the modal, dialog, or inline form where new logs are created), make these specific changes: BEFORE: The weather fields (conditions, temperature high, temperature low, wind) are empty text/number inputs that require manual entry. There is no mechanism to copy data from a previous day log. AFTER: 1) Import or use the existing weather hook (check for useWeather in src/hooks/) to fetch current weather data for the project. On form mount, auto-populate the weather_description, temperature_high, and temperature_low fields from the weather cache data. 2) Next to each auto-populated field, render a small badge: a span with text 'Auto', background color '#E8F5E9', color '#2E7D32', fontSize 10px, borderRadius 4px, padding '2px 6px', fontWeight 600. 3) Allow the user to override auto-populated values by simply editing the field. When edited, remove the 'Auto' badge. 4) Add a button labeled 'Same as yesterday' (secondary style: white background, 1px gray border, dark text) at the top of the form. When clicked, fetch the most recent daily log for this project and pre-fill crew entries, equipment, and visitor fields from that log. Show a toast 'Pre-filled from previous log'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety incident creation form lacks inline validation and required field indicators
issues.description: The safety incident creation form does not validate on blur, does not show red asterisks on required fields (date, location, description, severity), and does not show inline error messages below invalid fields. Design Standards require inline validation on blur, red asterisk after required labels, and red border with error text below on validation failure. This also violates PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Safety criterion 4.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, in the incident creation form (the modal or form component used to create new safety incidents), make these specific changes: BEFORE: Required fields (date, location, description, severity) have no visual required indicator. Validation only occurs on submit (if at all), with no per-field feedback. Input borders do not change on validation failure. AFTER: 1) Add a red asterisk after each required field label. Use a span with style: { color: '#E74C3C', marginLeft: 2 } containing '*'. Required fields are: date, location, description, severity, and involved_persons. 2) Add onBlur validation to each required field. When a required field loses focus and is empty, set its border to '1px solid #E74C3C' and render a paragraph below it with style { color: '#E74C3C', fontSize: 12, marginTop: 4 } containing the message (e.g., 'Location is required'). When the field has a valid value, clear the error state and reset border to '1px solid #E5E7EB'. 3) For the severity dropdown, validate that a value is selected (not the default placeholder). 4) On form submit, check all required fields. If any are invalid, prevent submission, focus the first invalid field, and show a toast 'Please complete all required fields'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore Quick Capture allows video-to-punch-item with automatic transcription and has strong mobile offline support. Fieldwire excels with true offline capability and Apple Pencil markup on drawings. SiteSync field operations currently lag both competitors in mobile responsiveness, offline queuing, and speed of field data entry. The Daily Log lacks the sub-90-second submission target that would match Fieldwire usability. Safety module is functional but lacks OSHA 300 form generation and the inspection template system that Procore provides.
summary: Field Operations scores 33/100, held back primarily by missing loading/error/empty states across Daily Log, Field Capture, and Safety pages, poor mobile responsiveness with overflowing grids and undersized touch targets, missing ARIA attributes throughout, and incomplete domain workflows like weather auto-population and same-as-yesterday log cloning. The five surgical fixes target the highest-impact gaps: adding proper loading/error/empty states to Daily Log (the most-used field page), adding ARIA and keyboard support to Field Capture, making Safety responsive on mobile, wiring weather auto-population into Daily Log creation, and adding form validation to safety incident creation. These fixes address the Global Acceptance Standards violations that currently make every field operations page fail the PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA before page-specific criteria are even evaluated.
